text: The surgeon performs an EDL biopsy on a suspicious nodule in the left upper lobe, followed by the appearance of a large amount of blood within the chest cavity.
The surgeon retracts the lung anteriorly, revealing a large aortic hematoma with active bleeding from the descending thoracic aorta.
The surgeon achieves initial bleeding control through compression before converting the procedure to an anterior thoracotomy, exposing the chest cavity for further intervention.
The post-operative CT shows the presence of a large hematoma. The patient was eventually discharged without any further intervention.
